content: I will replace the vacancy registration overlay code block in `CompanyDashboard.tsx` with a two-column grid layout where labels are right-aligned on the left and input fields are aligned on the right. I will also apply the translucent white background with backdrop blur (`bg-white/95 backdrop-blur-md`), change active elements to use the brand purple `#533af6`, add the purple underline left-aligned below the title, and change all inputs and selects to `rounded-[5px]`.
thinking:
**Refining Code Block**

I'm now focusing intensely on the `replace_file_content` task for the vacancy registration overlay. Currently, I'm meticulously crafting the new code for the block, paying close attention to detail to ensure its accuracy and to eliminate any potential typos. I want it to be perfect, as I replace the section between lines 3009 and 3606.


**Constructing Overlay**

I'm now focusing on building the vacancy registration overlay UI, especially the step header with its progress indicators. I'm leveraging motion for smooth transitions between steps and implementing a clean design with clear visual cues for each step. The progress dots, and header information are being coded carefully to create an intuitive user experience.


**Designing UI Elements**

I'm now deep in the weeds of crafting the vacancy registration form's UI, focusing intently on the input fields. I'm leveraging Tailwind classes to build a modern, responsive design.  I'm paying close attention to the input field styling, particularly the modal inputs, to ensure a cohesive and user-friendly experience, while carefully managing state.


**Implementing UI Elements**

I'm now in the thick of implementing form elements.  I'm leveraging Tailwind classes to build the state and city select components with their respective options, including state and city selection, along with a remuneration type selection and proposed salary field. I am also working on their respective change handlers and disable conditions, and also setting up the visual styling, ensuring a clean and consistent design throughout.


**Refining Bonus Input**

I'm now refining the bonus input field within the vacancy form. I'm focusing on the logic that controls the visibility and behavior of the bonus type and value inputs.  I've incorporated a checkbox to toggle the display and working on dynamic rendering, ensuring the related fields appear only when the bonus is activated, and am also focusing on their respective change handlers and disable conditions, and setting up v
<truncated 133 bytes>
form elements. I'm focused on the work type dropdown, work schedule dropdown, and the first job toggle, including both visual and functional elements. I've also incorporated the minimum age range slider.  I am also setting up the benefits, incorporating selection logic and state management. I am carefully setting up the `onChange` event handlers, ensuring that they properly update the form state.


**Implementing Benefits**

I'm now implementing the benefits section in the form, specifically focusing on the conditional rendering and state management. I'm leveraging a map function to dynamically generate benefit buttons based on a dataset. I am integrating value inputs for those benefits with values, and setting up the event handlers. I'm also addressing extra benefits, allowing users to add and remove custom benefits using a button to remove elements. I've also incorporated a close icon for deletion. I'm ensuring all is properly handled.


**Implementing Requirements**

I'm now implementing the additional requirement functionality, and have recently added the extra benefit functionality. I'm focusing on the implementation of text inputs and buttons to add custom requirements to the vacancy form, specifically ensuring that when the 'Enter' key is pressed or the plus button clicked, the new requirement is added to the form's state. I'm also ensuring the input field clears after each addition, and that it can handle common requirements. The user can also remove custom benefits.



**Adding Stage Functionality**

I'm now implementing the stage funnel feature, and have been implementing the new features in the form. I'm building text inputs and buttons to add custom funnel stages to the vacancy form. I'm ensuring that when the 'Enter' key is pressed or the plus button clicked, the new stage is added to the form's state. I'm also ensuring the input field clears after each addition, and that it can handle common stages. The user can also remove custom stages. I've added error handling to prohibit certain stage names.

args.Description: "Redesign vacancy registration modal with two-column aligned layout and purple theme in CompanyDashboard.tsx"
args.Instruction: "Replace the entire modal overlay container for vacancy registration to match the aligned label inputs, purple accents, and semi-transparent blur layout in CompanyDashboard.tsx"
args.ReplacementContent:
" {/* Step Header - Compact & Premium */}

CADASTRAR NOVA VAGA

Passo {registerStep} de 3

{/* Step Progress Dots */}
{[1, 2, 3].map((step) => (
step ? 'bg-[#533af6]/85 text-white' : 'bg-slate-200 text-slate-400' }`}> {registerStep > step ? : step}
args.TargetContent:
" {/* Step Header - Compact */}

Publicar Vaga

Passo {registerStep} de 3

{/* Step Progress - More subtle */}
{[1, 2, 3].map((step) => (
step ? 'bg-emerald-500 text-white' : 'bg-slate-200 text-slate-400' }`}>
args.toolAction: "Replacing complete vacancy registration overlay container with two-column aligned form layout in CompanyDashboard.tsx"
